previous_statement: (Dimension calculation). The self-adjoint part of the commutant has real dimension: dim_R(π_φ(A)'^{sa}) = dim_R((⊕_{j=1}^k M_{n_j}(C))^{sa}) = Σ_{j=1}^k dim_R(M_{n_j}(C)^{sa}) = Σ_{j=1}^k n_j² (since the self-adjoint (Hermitian) n_j × n_j matrices form a real vector space of dimension n_j²). Combining with the bound from Node 1.3: Σ_{j=1}^k n_j² ≤ m + 1. This is part (II) of the main theorem.
new_statement: (Dimension calculation — amended). By Node 1.4.2 (Artin-Wedderburn): π_φ(A)' ≅ ⊕_{j=1}^k M_{n_j}(C). The self-adjoint part of each block M_{n_j}(C)^{sa} consists of n_j × n_j Hermitian matrices, a real vector space of dimension n_j². Therefore: dim_R(π_φ(A)'^{sa}) = Σ_{j=1}^k dim_R(M_{n_j}(C)^{sa}) = Σ_{j=1}^k n_j². Combining with Node 1.3 (dim_R(π_φ(A)'^{sa}) ≤ m + 1): Σ_{j=1}^k n_j² ≤ m + 1. Combined with Node 1.4.3 (the n_j are the representation multiplicities), this yields part (II) of the main theorem. This is a computation/deduction depending on Nodes 1.4.2, 1.4.3, and 1.3.
